tools.name: future_value
tools.description: Calculate future value of an investment with compound interest. Use this for investment growth projections, savings account growth, and retirement planning. For comparing multiple rates and periods, use future_value_table instead.

rate.description: Annual interest rate as decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%)

years.description: Number of years for the investment

tools.name: present_value
tools.description: Calculate present value (current worth) of a future amount. Use this for loan valuation, bond pricing, and investment analysis. For comparing multiple scenarios, use future_value_table with different periods and rates instead.

rate.description: Annual discount rate as decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%)

years.description: Number of years until the amount is received

tools.name: discount
tools.description: Calculate final price after applying a percentage discount. Use this for retail pricing, sale calculations, and cost savings analysis. For comparing multiple discount percentages, use discount_table instead.

discount.description: Discount percentage (e.g., 20 for 20% or 0.20 as decimal, depending on input format)

tools.name: markup
tools.description: Calculate selling price from cost and markup percentage. Use this for product pricing, profit margin calculation, and business pricing strategy. For comparing multiple markup scenarios, use discount_table which also shows profit margins.

markup.description: Markup percentage (e.g., 30 for 30% or 0.30 as decimal, depending on input format)

tools.name: future_value_table
tools.description: Generate a comparison table of future values across multiple interest rates and time periods. Use this for investment scenario comparison, rate shopping, and long-term planning. For single calculations, use future_value instead.

periods.description: Time periods in years to compare (e.g., [1, 5, 10, 20])

tools.name: discount_table
tools.description: Compare multiple discount percentages for the same price. Use this for bulk pricing strategies, promotional planning, and price comparison. For single discount calculations, use discount instead.

tools.name: launch_ui
tools.description: Launch the interactive web UI for the financial calculator. Use this for visual, tab-based calculations with real-time results and beautiful gradient UI. For command-line calculations, use the individual calculation tools instead.

port.description: Port number for the web server (default: 5050)

messages.content: Hey there! I've been thinking about my retirement planning and I wanted to explore something. I have some book royalties that I haven't touched yet, and I'm considering investing them for the long term. Could you help me figure out how to calculate the future value of those royalties if I invest them as retirement savings? I'd love to understand how different interest rates and time periods might affect the growth.

I'd be happy to help you explore how your book royalties could grow over time! To use the future value calculator, I'll need a few details from you:

1. **The amount** - How much are the book royalties you plan to invest? (Even an approximate number works)

2. **Interest rates** - Any specific rates you'd like to compare? (e.g., 5%, 7%, 10%)

3. **Time periods** - How many years are you planning for retirement? (e.g., 10, 20, 30 years)

If you're not sure about the rates or periods, I can use some common defaults (like comparing 4%, 6%, 8%, and 10% over 10, 20, and 30 years) to give you a helpful overview.

What numbers would you like to work with?

That sounds helpful for the retirement planning! I'll think about those numbers and get back to you.

By the way, I've also been working on creating an online course and I want to make sure I price it properly. Do you know how to calculate markup pricing for a course based on what it costs to produce? I'd like to figure out a good pricing strategy that covers my costs while staying fair for my audience.

Absolutely! I can help you with that using the markup pricing calculator. To figure out a fair price for your course, I'll need a couple of things:

1. **Production cost** - What does it cost to create the course? This could include:
   - Equipment (camera, mic, lighting)
   - Software/editing tools
   - Any contractors (editors, designers)
   - Marketing or platform fees

2. **Markup percentage** - What margin would you like to apply? (This is the percentage you add on top of your costs)

For reference, online course markups typically range from **50% to 200%** depending on the value provided, but you can choose whatever makes sense for your situation.

Once you give me the cost and your desired markup, I can calculate the selling price for you. Would you also like to compare a few different markup scenarios to see a range of possible prices?

That sounds great! Let's go ahead with those defaults you mentioned - comparing 4%, 6%, 8%, and 10% over 10, 20, and 30 years. I'd love to see that comparison table to understand how different rates and time periods can really make a difference in growing the investment over time. Can you generate that for me?

I'd love to generate that comparison table for you! However, I'm missing one key piece of information:

**How much are the book royalties** you plan to invest? (The starting principal amount)

Once you give me that number, I can create the full comparison table showing how your investment could grow at 4%, 6%, 8%, and 10% over 10, 20, and 30 years. This will really illustrate the power of compound interest over time!

Oh, before we wrap up the investment projections, I've also been thinking about promotional pricing for my book bundles. I'm planning a sale campaign and want to figure out different discount scenarios - like what happens if I offer 10%, 15%, or 20% off, or maybe bundle a few books together at a special price. Could you help me explore those discount options?

Absolutely, I can help you explore those discount scenarios for your book bundles! I'll need one piece of information:

**What is the regular price** of the book bundle(s) you plan to put on sale?

Once you give me that, I can show you exactly what the sale price would be at 10%, 15%, 20%, or any other discount percentage you're considering. This will help you see how each discount level affects your revenue and what might work best for your promotional campaign.

Sure! The book bundle I'm planning to put on sale is currently priced at $75. I'd love to see what the sale prices would be at those different discount levels - 10%, 15%, and 20% off. That would really help me plan my promotional campaign and see how much revenue I'd be working with at each level.
Let me generate that discount comparison table for you right away!

Here's what your book bundle sale prices would look like at each discount level:

| Discount | Sale Price | Savings per Bundle |
|----------|------------|-------------------|
| **10% off** | $67.50 | $7.50 |
| **15% off** | $63.75 | $11.25 |
| **20% off** | $60.00 | $15.00 |

This gives you a clear picture of how each discount level impacts both the customer's savings and your revenue per bundle. For example:

- At 10% off, you'd keep $67.50 per bundle
- At 20% off, you'd keep $60.00 per bundle

It's worth considering how many additional sales the discount might generate — a bigger discount could attract more buyers, offsetting the lower per-unit revenue.

Let me know if you'd like to explore any other discount percentages or if you're ready to circle back to those investment projections for your royalties!
